* [Anna Hübler](/wiki/Anna_H%C3%BCbler "Anna Hübler") * [Heinrich Burger](/wiki/Heinrich_Burger "Heinrich Burger")[[4]](#cite_note-4) | [Germany](/wiki/Germany_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Germany at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | 10 (1) | 12 (1) | 11 (1) | 12 (1) | 11 (1) | 11.20 | 5

* [Phyllis Johnson](/wiki/Phyllis_Johnson "Phyllis Johnson") * [James H. Johnson](/wiki/James_H._Johnson_(figure_skater) "James H. Johnson (figure skater)") | [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | 9 (2) | 11.5 (2) | 10.5 (2) | 10.5 (2) | 10 (2) | 10.30 | 10[[5]](#cite_note-Ordinals-5)

* [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers") * [Edgar Syers](/wiki/Edgar_Syers "Edgar Syers") | [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | 9 (2) | 11.5 (2) | 10 (3) | 8 (3) | 9.5 (3) | 9.60 | 13

was one of four events in figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics. Each nation could enter up to 3 pairs (6 skaters).[[1]](#cite_note-OR39-1) Twelve years later, silver medalist [Phyllis Johnson](/wiki/Phyllis_Johnson "Phyllis Johnson") would capture a bronze medal with a new partner at the [1920 Olympics](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1920 Summer Olympics").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* [![United Kingdom](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/a/ae/Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_Kingdom.svg.png)](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") [Herbert G. Fowler](/w/index.php?title=Herbert_G._Fowler&action=edit&redlink=1 "Herbert G. Fowler (page does not exist)")
["Figure Skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics: Pairs, Mixed"](https://www.olympedia.org/results/12542). *Olympedia*. Retrieved 1 July 2020.

Hines, James R. (2011). *Historical Dictionary of Figure Skating*. Lanham, Maryland: Scarecrow Press. pp. xxi. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-8108-6859-5](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-6859-5 "Special:BookSources/978-0-8108-6859-5").

The Official Report gives the total ordinals for the silver medal pair as 10 and for the bronze medal pair as 13, in each case counting the two judges who awarded a tie for 2nd as being 2 ordinals. Other sources give both pairs a 2.5 for those ties, resulting in total ordinals of 11 and 14.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Figure skating at the Olympics

| Pairs figure skating  at the Games of the IV Olympiad | |

Johnson](/wiki/James_H._Johnson_(figure_skater) "James H. Johnson (figure skater)") / [Phyllis Johnson](/wiki/Phyllis_Johnson "Phyllis Johnson") | [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | | 3rd place, bronze medalist(s) |  | [Edgar Syers](/wiki/Edgar_Syers "Edgar Syers") / [Madge Syers](/wiki/Madge_Syers "Madge Syers") | [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | | |

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Template:Figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Template talk:Figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Special:EditPage/Template:Figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | | |

The **mixed pairs** was one of four events in figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics. Each nation could enter up to 3 pairs (6 skaters).[[1]](#cite_note-OR39-1) Twelve years later, silver medalist [Phyllis Johnson](/wiki/Phyllis_Johnson "Phyllis Johnson") would capture a bronze medal with a new partner at the [1920 Olympics](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1920_Summer_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1920 Summer Olympics").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Competition format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Pairs&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Competition format")]

Each skater performed a five-minute free skate. Scores from 0 to 6 were given for each figure for both (a) content (difficulty and variety) and (b) performance. The total possible score was therefore 12. Each judge would then arrange the pairs in order of total score by that judge; these ordinal rankings were used to provide final placement for the pairs, using a "majority rule"--if a majority of the judges ranked a pair first, the pair won. If there was no majority, the total ordinals controlled. Ties were broken by total points.[[3]](#cite_note-OR558to65-3)

## Results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Pairs&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Results")]

The judges were unanimous in ranking the German pair first. For second place, two judges scored a tie between the Johnsons and the Syerses, while three judges put the Johnsons clearly in second; this being a majority, the Johnsons received the silver medal.

Johnson (figure skater)") | [Great Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Great Britain at the 1908 Summer Olympics") | 9 (2) | 11.5 (2) | 10.5 (2) | 10.5 (2) | 10 (2) | 10.30 | 10[[5]](#cite_note-Ordinals-5) |

Other sources give both pairs a 2.5 for those ties, resulting in total ordinals of 11 and 14.
